reference: WE WOMEN ARE ONLY AS OLD AS WE LOOK AND THE LEECHES AND TIRING WOMEN OF THIS BEAUTY OF FORTY PRACTISE ARTS WHICH GIVE HER THE APPEARANCE OF TWENTY FIVE YET PERHAPS THE KING VALUES HER INTELLECT MORE THAN HER PERSON AND THE WISDOM OF A HUNDRED SERPENTS IS CERTAINLY UNITED IN THIS WOMAN'S HEAD
text: We women are only as old as we look, and the leeches and tiring-women of this beauty of forty practice arts which give her the appearance of twenty-five. Yet perhaps the king values her intellect more than her person, and the wisdom of a hundred serpents is certainly united in this woman's head.
